rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component type features
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain scope consistency by ensuring annex blocks are placed in the correct component or implementation context
Preserve existing architecture by not modifying component types or implementations beyond annex insertion
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Ensure all referenced identifiers in annex blocks are visible in the target scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component type features
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in component type annexes that reference implementation-only subcomponent paths
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax notes for assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign
Type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
